items.q: What freight fits in a 53-foot trailer?
items.a: Full truckload shipments (12+ pallets). Long-haul linehaul between distribution centers.

items.q: How many pallets can a 53-foot trailer carry?
items.a: A 53-foot trailer typically handles 26-30 pallets with a max weight capacity of 42,000-45,000 lbs.

items.q: When should I NOT use a 53-foot trailer?
items.a: Small loads under 6 pallets (overpaying for space). Locations without dock-height receiving.
Yes. WARP operates a fleet that includes 53-foot trailer capacity, matched to shipment size through our right-sized asset approach.

rendered.body_text:
Standard full-size dry van trailer for high-volume linehaul and FTL freight

A 53-foot trailer handles 26-30 pallets with a capacity of 42,000-45,000 lbs. Interior dimensions: 53 ft length, 9 ft height.

Best fit for: Full truckload shipments (12+ pallets); Long-haul linehaul between distribution centers.

Get an instant 53-foot trailer freight rate at wearewarp.com/quote.
rendered.primary_content_html:
53-Foot Trailer Overview

Standard full-size dry van trailer for high-volume linehaul and FTL freight

Mode: FTL

53-Foot Trailer Specifications

SpecificationValue
Capacity Pallets26-30
Capacity Lbs42,000-45,000
Interior Length Ft53
Interior Height Ft9
Door Typeswing doors, dock-height

Best Fit Freight for 53-Foot Trailer

  • Full truckload shipments (12+ pallets)
  • Long-haul linehaul between distribution centers
  • Pool distribution linehaul legs
  • High-volume committed lane freight

When to Choose a Different Vehicle

  • Small loads under 6 pallets (overpaying for space)
  • Locations without dock-height receiving
  • Last-mile residential delivery
  • Urban areas with vehicle length restrictions

Solutions Using This Equipment

  • Pool Distribution — Consolidated freight pooling from distribution centers to regional delivery zones
  • Vendor Consolidation — Multi-vendor pickup consolidation into single linehaul shipments
  • Zone Skipping — Bypass carrier sort hubs by injecting freight closer to final delivery zones

Related Network Capabilities

  • Middle Mile — The linehaul segment between origin hub and destination hub before final delivery
  • Cross-Docking — Direct transfer of freight between inbound and outbound vehicles without warehousing
  • Predictable Pricing — Consistent rate structures based on lane commitment and volume patterns
  • Right-Sized Assets — Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency

Ship with 53-Foot Trailer Today

Get Instant Quote Book a Fit Call
